sample_queries: What are the side effects of ibuprofen?
How should I take my blood pressure medication?
What does an elevated white blood cell count mean?
sample_ground_truth: Ibuprofen may cause stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness. Serious side effects include stomach bleeding and kidney problems.
Take blood pressure medication as prescribed, usually once daily with water. Avoid missing doses.
An elevated white blood cell count may indicate infection, inflammation, or other medical conditions requiring further evaluation.

sample_documents.text: Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to treat pain and inflammation. Common side effects include stomach upset and dizziness.
